questions.text: Who founded the Brahmo Samaj in Calcutta?
questions.explanation: Raja Rammohan Roy, a social reformer, founded the Brahmo Sabha (later Samaj) in 1828.

questions.text: The practice of 'Sati' was officially banned in which year?
questions.explanation: Raja Rammohan Roy's efforts led to the banning of Sati by Lord William Bentinck in 1829.

questions.text: Which reformer used ancient texts to suggest that widows could remarry?
questions.explanation: Vidyasagar argued for widow remarriage, leading to the 1856 Widow Remarriage Act.

questions.text: The 'Arya Samaj' was founded by:
questions.explanation: Dayanand Saraswati founded the Arya Samaj in 1875 to reform Hinduism.

questions.text: In the Telugu-speaking areas of the Madras Presidency, who formed an association for widow remarriage?
questions.explanation: Veerasalingam Pantulu was a pioneer of social reform in Andhra.

questions.text: Who established the Mohammedan Anglo-Oriental College at Aligarh?
questions.explanation: Founded in 1875, it later became the Aligarh Muslim University.

questions.text: Who wrote 'Stripurushtulna', a book comparing women and men?
questions.explanation: Tarabai Shinde published this critique of the social differences between men and women.

questions.text: Pandita Ramabai founded a shelter for ________ in Poona.
questions.explanation: She established a home for widows who had been treated badly by their husbands' relatives.

questions.text: The Child Marriage Restraint Act was passed in ________.
questions.explanation: It set the minimum age of marriage at 18 for boys and 16 for girls (later changed).

questions.text: Which movement was started by Ghasidas to improve the social status of leather workers in Central India?
questions.explanation: Ghasidas founded the Satnami movement to reform the status of leather workers.

questions.text: Who gave the famous slogan 'One caste, one religion, one god for humankind'?
questions.explanation: Narayana Guru, from Kerala, preached the unity of all humans.

questions.text: Who founded the 'Satyashodhak Samaj'?
questions.explanation: Jyotirao Phule founded the 'Truth-Seekers Society' to advocate for low castes.

questions.text: Phule dedicated his book 'Gulamgiri' to the people of which country?
questions.explanation: He dedicated it to the Americans who fought to free slaves in the American Civil War.

questions.text: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar started the ________ movement in 1927 to challenge caste prejudice.
questions.explanation: He led three such movements between 1927 and 1935 to assert the rights of Dalits.

questions.text: Who was popularly known as 'Periyar'?
questions.explanation: Periyar was a radical social reformer from Tamil Nadu.

questions.text: Periyar started the ________ movement in the early 20th century.
questions.explanation: He argued that untouchables were the true upholders of an original Tamil culture.

questions.text: The 'Veda Samaj' was established in ________ in 1864.
questions.explanation: Inspired by the Brahmo Samaj, it worked for social and religious reform in South India.

questions.text: The Prarthana Samaj was established in 1867 in:
questions.explanation: Prarthana Samaj focused on removing caste restrictions and promoting women's education.

questions.text: Who established the Singh Sabhas for Sikh reform?
questions.explanation: The first Singh Sabha was formed in Amritsar in 1873.

questions.text: What was the main aim of the Satyashodhak Samaj?
questions.explanation: It aimed at freeing low-caste people from the dominance of Brahmans.

questions.text: Tarabai Shinde was educated at ________.
questions.explanation: She was educated at home in Poona by her father.

questions.text: Mumtaz Ali reinterpreted verses from the ________ to argue for women's education.
questions.explanation: She used Islamic scripture to support the education of Muslim women.

questions.text: The Paramhans Mandali was founded in 1840 to work for the:
questions.explanation: Its members often met in secret and broke caste taboos.

questions.text: Who among these were known as 'untouchables'?
questions.explanation: Mahars were a significant Dalit community in Maharashtra.

questions.text: Jyotirao Phule belonged to which caste?
questions.explanation: He was from a non-Brahman low-caste background.

questions.text: In 1873, Phule wrote a book named:
questions.explanation: Gulamgiri means 'Slavery'.

questions.text: Which community worked in plantations in Assam and Mauritius as laborers?
questions.explanation: Poverty and caste oppression drove many low-caste people to seek work abroad.

questions.text: The Madigas were an important untouchable caste in present-day:
questions.explanation: They were expert tanners and leather workers.

questions.text: Which reformer was critical of the national movement led by upper castes?
questions.explanation: Periyar believed the Congress was dominated by Brahmans who would maintain caste hierarchy.

questions.text: Savitribai Phule was associated with the start of ________ in Maharashtra.
questions.explanation: Along with her husband Jyotirao, she opened the first school for girls in Pune.

questions.text: Pandita Ramabai was a great scholar of ________.
questions.explanation: Her knowledge of Sanskrit was legendary, earning her the title 'Pandita'.

questions.text: Who established schools for Muslim girls in Patna and Calcutta?
questions.explanation: She was a fearless critic of conservative ideas among Muslims.

questions.text: Raja Rammohan Roy published a newspaper named:
questions.explanation: He used the press to spread his reformist ideas.

questions.text: Dayanand Saraswati believed that Hinduism should return to the:
questions.explanation: His motto was 'Go back to the Vedas'.

questions.text: In which century did most social reforms begin in India?
questions.explanation: The 19th century is often called the 'age of social reform'.

questions.text: Which organization was founded by Keshab Chandra Sen?
questions.explanation: He was a dynamic leader who expanded the Brahmo Samaj.

questions.text: Which group of people were often forced to live outside villages?
questions.explanation: Untouchables faced severe residential segregation.

questions.text: Who said that 'Brahmans were foreigners' in India?
questions.explanation: Phule argued that Brahmans were descendants of Aryans who came from outside and defeated the original inhabitants.

questions.text: The Temple Entry movement was mostly focused on entry into ________ temples.
questions.explanation: It aimed at ending the exclusion of Dalits from Hindu places of worship.

questions.text: The first school for girls in North India was established in Aligarh by:
questions.explanation: He believed education was the key to progress for the Muslim community.
